records.note: According to Duotrope this place accepts 33% of their submissions.

From Michelle Wittle:Dear Poet:ￊI am writing today on behalf of Rosemary Cappello, Editor of Philadelphia Poets, to ask you to submit your poems for possible publication in Philadelphia Poets Volume 20 due out in Spring 2014. Rosemary was very impressed with your work when she met you back in October at the speed dating round at Push to Publish and would like to give you the first opportunity to possibly have your work published in the Spring 2014 issue. The volumes do fill up fast, so please do not wait too long to send your work.ￊSubmissions will open up June, 2013. Please see the submission guidelines attached to this email. If you have any further questions, please feel free to email Rosemary or myself. We are happy to help.ￊBest wishes to you,ￊMM Wittleￊￊￊ-- MM Wittle, Assistant Editor, Philadelphia Poetshttp://www.facebook/MMWittle
Email: redrose108@comcast.net

Submitted "The Great Symphony" via email to peekthemorpholux@yahoo.com

Dear Thomas, Thank you for submitting Robert Frost Helps Me Talk to My Father to Bartleby Snopes. We appreciate the chance to read your work. Unfortunately, this piece is not for us. We tend to prefer stories with more dramatic tension and more fully developed characters. This is more of anecdote. It needs more substance to carry the narrative, such as more demonstrated tension and conflict between the father and son and clarification about their relationship. Good luck with this one elsewhere. Feel free to try us again in the future with something new, but please wait at least one month before submitting again. Sincerely, The Editors Bartleby Snopes www.bartlebysnopes.comYou can go here to view the submission:http://bartlebysnopes.submittable.com/user/submissions/3860190

Paper only:	ﾥ	Typed, double-spaced, on one of 8.5ￓ x 11ￓ paper	ﾥ	Include cover letter (one page limit): name, address, phone number, email address, title of piece, and approximate word count	ﾥ	8,000 word limit for general submissions	ﾥ	Do not send disks or electronic files	ﾥ	Multiple submissions not accepted from individual authors (non-contest submissions only)	ﾥ	Simultaneous submissions require immediate notification to Fourth Genre if accepted elsewhereThey respond via email. Do not send SASE.

This magazine consistently has authors featured in national anthologies. It is among the best literary publications in existence. Takes online submissions.

Consistently the best publisher of poets in the country. They are always at the top of the list. To give you some idea of this magazines influence, an author from this journal has had work included in Best American Poetry 72 times in the 19 years BAP has been in publication. It is second only to The New Yorker (keep in mind The New Yorker hand picks most of its poems and has no unsolicited submissions).

Has consistently published some of the best writers in the world. They are the presenters of the Faulkner Award among others. The magazine has won every important national award, and it is always gaining attention for writers.
Consistently one of the best literary magazines in the country.
Has been included in numerous anthologies each year. It publishes new and established writers.
Consistently wins awards for authors and for itself. The magazine always publishes great work.
Has been featured in Best American Poetry over 20 times. The magazine consistently catches attention for its authors.

Consistently publishing great authors from the heart of a writers central. Some may not know that the Iowa Writerￕs Workshop is and has been the best writing program in the country for the last, at least, 40 years.
The oldest and one of the best literary publications in the country.
Simply one of the best literary magazines in the world. They have been publishing great authors for many years.
Has a great has a seemingly ever-growing appeal. They consistently publish outstanding authors.
A great publication. They publish the best authors and you always seem to see some big names gracing the pages.
